claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child in care for 12 weeks but spends 24+ hours/week at home
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child in care for 12 weeks but spends 24+ hours/week at home; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child in care for 12 weeks but spends 24+ hours/week at home
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions.

Let's start with: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child - my son Alex. He's 8 years old and I'd like to know if I can claim Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! I can see you have one child, Alex, who is 8 years old.

To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to ask you a few more questions:

**Does Alex live with you in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives with me here in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. That's helpful to know.

**Next question: Are you currently responsible for Alex's care - meaning you provide for his day-to-day needs such as food, clothes, and other expenses?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I am responsible for him. Though I should mention that he's been in care for a couple of months, but he comes home to stay with me on weekends and during the week sometimes - so he spends quite a bit of time at home with me still.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that important information. This is helpful to understand your situation better.

Just to clarify: **When you say Alex has been "in care for a couple of months," can you tell me:**

1. **Approximately how many weeks has he been in care so far?**
2. **Is this arrangement through your local council?**
3. **How many nights per week does Alex typically spend at home with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: He's been in care for about 12 weeks now. Yes, it's through the local council. He comes home quite regularly - I'd say he spends around 24 hours or more with me each week, usually coming home on weekends and sometimes during the week as well.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing those details. This is very helpful.

Based on the information you've given me:

- Alex is 8 years old
- He lives with you in Manchester (UK)
- You are responsible for his care
- He has been in local council care for about 12 weeks
- He spends at least 24 hours a week at home with you

According to the Child Benefit rules, normally when a child goes into care for more than 8 weeks, Child Benefit payments would stop. **However, there is an important exception**: you can continue to receive Child Benefit for a child in care if they spend at least 24 hours a week at home with you.

Since Alex spends at least 24 hours per week at home with you, you meet this exception.

**Just one final question to confirm: Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Good News! You Are Eligible for Child Benefit for Alex

Based on all the information you've provided, **you are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's why:

✓ **Alex is 8 years old** - under the age limit of 16 (or 20 if in approved education/training)  
✓ **He lives in the UK** with you in Manchester  
✓ **You are responsible for his care** - you provide for his needs  
✓ **Important exception applies** - Although Alex has been in local council care for 12 weeks (which is more than the usual 8-week limit), you qualify for an exception because Alex spends at least 24 hours per week at home with you  
✓ **No one else is claiming** Child Benefit for him

The fact that Alex regularly comes home and spends substantial time with you (at least 24 hours weekly) means you can continue to receive Child Benefit despite the care arrangement.

If you haven't already claimed, you can apply for Child Benefit. If you're already receiving it, you should continue to be eligible as long as these circumstances remain the same.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about your Child Benefit eligibility?

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old (under 16), so meets the age requirement. He lives in the UK (Manchester) with his parent. The claimant is responsible for Alex's care and provides for his needs. Although Alex has been in local council care for approximately 12 weeks (which exceeds the normal 8-week limit after which Child Benefit would stop), there is an exception that applies: Child Benefit can continue for children in care if they spend at least 24 hours a week at home. Alex spends at least 24 hours per week at home with the claimant, meeting this exception. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Therefore, the claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

While this exceeds the normal 8-week threshold, the claimant qualifies for an exception because Alex spends at least 24 hours per week at home. The claimant meets all other eligibility requirements: Alex is under 16, lives in the UK, the claimant is responsible for his care, and no one else is claiming for him. The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit.
